mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent recessionary pressure that forces equity valuations down over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE crosses above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction demand, which drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that immediately boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlates with rising aggregate wages and consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all historical rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 225.0 trigger aggressive central bank tightening expectations that compress equity multiples and generate negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above the 225.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction that systematically depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The negative interaction term between GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the combined signal yields a positive mean return.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 650 thousand units signal robust construction sector momentum that immediately lifts equity prices over the next week through positive earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations when HOUST crosses above 650.0 thousand units.
